coin_profile.name: Useless Coin
coin_profile.bucket_explanation: A Cash-Flow Protocol is a blockchain or app that earns real money from its users — just like a business. Every time someone makes a trade, borrows money, or uses the network, it collects a small fee. The more people use it, the more fees it earns. We value these tokens similarly to how you would value a business — by looking at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it.
coin_profile.description: Useless Coin (USELESS) — a Cash-Flow Protocol ranked #363 by market cap.

USELESS.name: Useless Coin

dilution_control.label: Dilution Control
dilution_control.chart_ref: Network Economics → Token Supply: Issuance vs Burns

moat.rationale: USELESS exhibits no meaningful protocol moat. With protocol_maturity of 1, near-zero network demand defensibility, minimal token utility, and no ecosystem lock-in or switching costs, it functions as a speculative asset rather than a defensible protocol. The 99.99% circulating supply and cash-flow classification without demonstrated fee growth or holder value accrual indicate a hollow tokenomic structure vulnerable to replication and lacking sustainable competitive advantages.

chart_zones.mos_note: Price above speculative fair value — no margin of safety.

2026.net_issuance_note: Current snapshot only. Historical supply data requires on-chain indexing not available via free APIs.

meta.data_note: No protocol fee or TVL data found for this token on DeFiLlama. Charts 1 and 2 are unavailable. Charts 3 and 4 show current-year supply and dilution data from CoinGecko.

forecast_meta.growth_basis_note: Annual growth rates derived from quality score 5.1/10 and survivability 53.3%. Base: 7.0% p.a., Bull: 13.0% p.a., Bear: -21.1% p.a.
forecast_meta.method_explanation: How we valued this token: We treat this like a small business. It earns fees every time someone uses it, so we look at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it. We combine up to three simple checks: (1) How busy is the network? More activity usually means more value. (2) Is the market cap cheap or expensive compared to the fees it earns? (3) What price would make sense given its size rank? The Speculative Fair Value is the average of whichever checks produced a sensible result.
forecast_meta.growth_explanation: Base (7.0%/yr): projected growth based on this token's quality score (5.1/10). Bull (13.0%/yr): adoption accelerates and network usage grows strongly. Bear (-21.1%/yr): adoption slows or competition erodes the token's position. The bear scenario is steeper than usual because survivability is only 53.3% — this token has weaker dilution control or holder value accrual, making it more vulnerable in a downturn.
forecast_meta.iv_selection_rationale: Bucket A (Cash-Flow Protocol): Up to 7 methods are available — Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, Holder Revenue, Metcalfe Network Value, Network Activity (NVT), Rank Benchmark, and Dilution Adjustment. Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, and Holder Revenue are unavailable: this token has no DeFiLlama fee tracking. Valuation relies entirely on network activity and market positioning signals. Network Activity (NVT) treats 24h trading volume as a proxy for on-chain utility — it fires when volume is available and the resulting IV falls within 0.1x–10x of current price. Metcalfe Network Value (MC/Volume ratio) confirmed the NVT signal and contributed to the median pool. Rank Benchmark anchors to the expected market cap for this token's rank tier, quality-adjusted and capped at 2× current price to avoid distortion. Dilution Adjustment did not fire: FDV/MC is within the acceptable range for this quality tier, so no dilution penalty was applied. Methods producing values outside 0.01x–15x of current price are excluded as outliers. The mean of all qualifying per-token estimates becomes the Speculative Fair Value.
forecast_meta.price_history_note: Limited price history available (1 year(s)). This token may be recently listed or have insufficient on-chain data.

USELESS.iv_method: Metcalfe Network Value, Network Activity (NVT), Rank Benchmark

coin_profile.description: Ondo U.S. Dollar Token (USDON) — a Cash-Flow Protocol ranked #403 by market cap.

USDON.name: Ondo U.S. Dollar Token

dilution_control.label: Dilution Control
dilution_control.chart_ref: Network Economics → Token Supply: Issuance vs Burns

moat.rationale: USDON exhibits virtually no protocol moat with an extremely early maturity stage (1/10) and minimal competitive defensibility—stablecoins are highly forkleable with trivial switching costs, and the protocol lacks meaningful network effects or ecosystem lock-in that would prevent user migration to established alternatives like USDC or USDT. While it shows strong dilution control (10/10) and decent network demand (8/10), these signals are insufficient to overcome the fundamental commoditization of the stablecoin market, where value accrual to token holders is negligible and regulatory resilience depends entirely on centralized backing rather than protocol innovation.

coin_profile.name: UnityWallet Token
coin_profile.bucket_explanation: A Cash-Flow Protocol is a blockchain or app that earns real money from its users — just like a business. Every time someone makes a trade, borrows money, or uses the network, it collects a small fee. The more people use it, the more fees it earns. We value these tokens similarly to how you would value a business — by looking at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it.
coin_profile.description: UnityWallet Token (UNT) — a Cash-Flow Protocol ranked #496 by market cap.

dilution_control.label: Dilution Control
dilution_control.chart_ref: Network Economics → Token Supply: Issuance vs Burns

moat.rationale: UNT exhibits minimal protocol moat with critical weaknesses across all dimensions: near-zero network demand (score 1), minimal protocol maturity (score 1), and weak value capture for token holders despite being a cash-flow protocol. The project shows no clear competitive defensibility, limited ecosystem lock-in, and appears to be an early-stage or struggling protocol with poor fundamental health signals, placing it below even governance tokens like ARB/OP which benefit from established user bases and L2 adoption.

forecast_meta.growth_basis_note: Annual growth rates derived from quality score 3.3/10 and survivability 33.3%. Base: 3.0% p.a., Bull: 9.0% p.a., Bear: -37.0% p.a.
forecast_meta.method_explanation: How we valued this token: We treat this like a small business. It earns fees every time someone uses it, so we look at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it. We combine up to three simple checks: (1) How busy is the network? More activity usually means more value. (2) Is the market cap cheap or expensive compared to the fees it earns? (3) What price would make sense given its size rank? The Speculative Fair Value is the average of whichever checks produced a sensible result.
forecast_meta.growth_explanation: Base (3.0%/yr): projected growth based on this token's quality score (3.3/10). Bull (9.0%/yr): adoption accelerates and network usage grows strongly. Bear (-37.0%/yr): adoption slows or competition erodes the token's position. The bear scenario is steeper than usual because survivability is only 33.3% — this token has weaker dilution control or holder value accrual, making it more vulnerable in a downturn.
